stakes.what_this_is: A 108-line Python walker (verify.py) that runs over skills/regime_audit_report/ and exits 0 iff its renderer file is present, parses, imports cleanly, and declares the constants + entry function a render-skill requires. regime_audit_report is a render skill (its claim is the contract stats.json -> report.md), not a collector — so this verifier checks renderer-shape, not collector-shape. Same family as the dashboard verifier above; different shape under the hood.
stakes.why_we_build_it: Same load-bearing rule from CLAUDE.md as the verifier-for-dashboard proposal: 'If a 3.0-under-0.4 cannot walk it, it is not 4.0.' Render skills are no exception — they make structural claims that need walker-based verification. The gap was auto-detected by the same skills/gap_audit/collectors/skill_without_verifier.py.
if_approved: Gap data point skill_without_verifier:756c48f... flips from live to resolved on the next gap_audit run.
Render skills get a structural-verifier precedent, parallel to the collector-skill precedent set by the dashboard verifier above.
Floor ratio inches up by one more 1.0 file.
Likely a paired decision with the dashboard verifier above — approving both is the consistent move.
if_rejected: Gap stays open. Render skills remain unchecked at the structural layer.
Implies the same policy signal as a reject on the dashboard verifier — paired-rejection for shape consistency.
Strengthens the case for parameterized-bundle-verifier as the path forward for this gap class.

connects_to.label: Foundation rule that motivates this proposal
Paired pending proposal — same shape family, different target (decide together)
Already-promoted proposal of same shape — precedent
